title: Sydney Police Arrest Two More in Grandfather Murder Case
description: NSW detectives make fresh arrests in the kidnapping and murder investigation of Chris Baghsarian.
lede: Two additional suspects have been arrested in connection with the alleged kidnapping and murder of Sydney grandfather Chris Baghsarian. The arrests follow a tactical police operation in Seven Hills this morning.

sections.heading: Morning Raid Yields Arrests
paragraphs: NSW Police tactical operations officers executed a search warrant at approximately 6:30am Wednesday in the Sydney suburb of Seven Hills. The operation resulted in the arrest of a man and woman who are now assisting detectives with their inquiries.

The arrests represent a significant development in the ongoing investigation into the alleged kidnapping and murder of Chris Baghsarian, which occurred last month. Police have not yet released the identities or ages of the newly arrested individuals.
The search warrant was part of Strike Force operations that have been working around the clock since the initial incident was reported to authorities.

sections.heading: The Original Crime
paragraphs: Chris Baghsarian, described by family as a devoted grandfather, was allegedly kidnapped and murdered in February 2026. The circumstances surrounding his disappearance initially puzzled investigators.
Police sources indicate the case has involved extensive forensic analysis and witness interviews. The victim's family has been cooperating fully with the investigation while grieving their loss.
The alleged crime has shocked the local Sydney community, where Baghsarian was known as a respected family man with deep community ties.

sections.heading: Investigation Timeline
paragraphs: The investigation began when Baghsarian was reported missing by concerned family members. Initial searches focused on his last known movements and associates.
Forensic evidence and witness testimony gradually built a case that transformed the missing person investigation into a homicide inquiry. Multiple locations across Sydney have been examined.
Today's arrests suggest police have identified additional suspects beyond any initial arrests, indicating the scope of the alleged conspiracy may be broader than first thought.

sections.heading: Police Operations Continue
paragraphs: NSW Police have indicated the investigation remains active with additional leads being pursued. The tactical nature of this morning's operation suggests authorities had specific intelligence.
Detectives from the State Crime Command's homicide squad continue to coordinate the investigation. They are appealing for any additional witnesses to come forward.
The case highlights the complex nature of modern criminal investigations, often requiring extensive resources and multi-agency cooperation to achieve successful prosecutions.

sections.heading: Community Impact
paragraphs: The alleged murder has deeply affected the local community where Baghsarian lived. Neighbors describe him as a quiet, family-oriented man who didn't deserve such a fate.
Local community leaders have called for increased police presence while expressing confidence in the investigation's progress. The arrests provide some hope for justice.
Family members have requested privacy during this difficult time while expressing gratitude for the police work that led to today's arrests.

sections.heading: Next Steps in Legal Process
paragraphs: The newly arrested individuals will undergo questioning as part of the standard legal process. Police will have a limited time to either charge or release them.
If charges are laid, the case will proceed through NSW courts where the full details of the alleged crime may be revealed publicly for the first time.
The investigation's outcome will likely provide closure for the victim's family and demonstrate the effectiveness of NSW Police's major crime investigation capabilities.

faqs.a: Chris Baghsarian was a Sydney grandfather who was allegedly kidnapped and murdered in February 2026. He was known as a devoted family man in his local community.

faqs.q: How many people have been arrested in this case?
faqs.a: At least two people were arrested in today's operation, bringing the total number of arrests in the case higher, though police have not specified the exact total.

faqs.q: Where did the latest arrests take place?
faqs.a: The arrests occurred in Seven Hills, a suburb in western Sydney, during a search warrant executed around 6:30am on Wednesday morning.

faqs.q: What is the current status of the investigation?
faqs.a: The investigation remains active with NSW Police homicide detectives continuing to pursue additional leads and evidence in the case.

faqs.q: When did the original crime occur?
faqs.a: The alleged kidnapping and murder of Chris Baghsarian took place in February 2026, with the investigation ongoing since that time.
